summary: Iteration 2 testing: All 15 backend tests pass (100%). Core UI flows working: login, dashboard, exercises (40), train (MediaPipe activo), progress, profile. Found 1 critical bug: wrong credentials silently closes auth modal without showing error message.

ui_bugs.issue: Wrong credentials (401) silently closes the login modal instead of showing error message. Root cause: axios interceptor in /app/frontend/src/lib/api.js calls window.location.href='/' on ANY 401 response, including failed login attempts - this redirects before the catch block can call setError(). Modal closes without user feedback.

action_items: Fix api.js interceptor: only call window.location.href='/' on 401 if a token IS present in localStorage (i.e., session expired). If no token, it means it's a login attempt and the error should propagate to the catch block normally. Fix: `if (error.response?.status === 401 && localStorage.getItem('auth_token')) { localStorage.removeItem... window.location.href='/' }` else just `return Promise.reject(error)`
critical_code_review_comments: api.js interceptor redirects to '/' on ALL 401s including login failures - should only redirect on session expiry (when auth_token exists). Fix: add `&& localStorage.getItem('auth_token')` condition before redirect.
server.py CORS: allow_origins=['*'] with allow_credentials=True is a security issue for production
No brute force protection on /api/auth/login endpoint
context_for_next_testing_agent: All flows working except auth error display. Fix api.js interceptor as described in action_items. After fix, test that wrong credentials shows error message inside modal (data-testid='auth-error'). All other flows (dashboard, exercises 40 cards, train MediaPipe activo, progress, profile) confirmed working.
rca_of_issue: Bug: api.js line 20 - interceptor catches ALL 401 responses including login failures and calls window.location.href='/' which reloads the page before the catch block in Landing.js AuthModal can call setError(). Fix: only redirect if auth_token exists in localStorage (indicating expired session, not failed login).
